summary: Machine policy for online font providers. Good for privacy-sensitive or offline-lean systems, but it prevents Windows from downloading font catalog data and online font data when needed.

setting.name: Online Font Providers
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can contact an online font provider to download font catalog data and, when needed, font data for rendering text. This policy decides whether that online font path is allowed.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable online font providers to keep Windows from contacting Microsoft's font service, reduce network-backed content, or force the device to rely only on locally installed fonts.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning this off reduces online content lookups, but some text may lose access to downloadable fonts and Windows will only enumerate locally installed fonts.
setting.professional_notes: GroupPolicy.admx maps the machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\EnableFontProviders with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The local ADML help text says disabled means Windows does not connect to an online font provider and only enumerates locally installed fonts.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the online font provider policy.

allowed_values.label: Online font providers disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not connect to an online font provider and only enumerates locally installed fonts.

allowed_values.label: Online font providers enabled
allowed_values.meaning: Windows can query an online font provider for catalog and font data when needed.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes EnableFontProviders = 0, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 and newer systems supporting the Network Fonts policy

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The local ADML help text says default behavior depends on the Windows edition when the policy is not configured.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Devices that may need downloadable font support
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal edition-dependent behavior for downloadable fonts.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to stop Windows from contacting an online font provider.
tradeoffs: Windows can keep its ordinary font-provider behavior for the edition.
No policy-based restriction is enforced for online font lookups.

recommended_profiles.label: Offline lean
intended_for: Devices that should rely only on local fonts
avoid_for: Setups that depend on downloadable font support

states.rationale: Disable online font providers so Windows only enumerates locally installed fonts.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to avoid online font catalog and font downloads.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the best chance of rendering text with downloadable fonts when they are needed.
tradeoffs: Some text rendering scenarios may lose access to fonts that would otherwise be downloaded.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft GroupPolicy.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows EnableFontProviders with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the Windows System policy key.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft GroupPolicy.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says disabling the policy prevents Windows from connecting to an online font provider and leaves it enumerating only locally installed fonts; not configured is edition-dependent.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes EnableFontProviders = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - EnableFontProviders policy
evidence.summary: GroupPolicy.admx shows EnableFontProviders under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System. The mirrored ExplainText says enabling the policy lets Windows download fonts and font catalog data from an online provider, while disabling it keeps Windows on locally-installed fonts only. EnabledValue=1 and DisabledValue=0 are explicit.

decision.why: Local Microsoft policy files document the exact font-provider policy surface, value semantics, and online-font behavior. The app writes the documented disable value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="EnableFontProviders" class="Machine" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System" valueName="EnableFontProviders">; GroupPolicy.adml: If you disable this policy setting, Windows does not connect to an online font provider and only enumerates locally-installed fonts.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact key and value name; local ADML confirms that disabling the policy blocks online font-provider access and limits enumeration to locally installed fonts. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-enablefontproviders-admx.
